title: Building Molecular Testing Capacity for Salmonella in Foodborne Disease Surveillance
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Foodborne     diseases     caused     by     Salmonella     remain     a     leading     public     health     threat     globally  ,   resulting   in   significant     morbidity  ,   mortality  ,   and     economic     losses  .   Traditional     culture-based     detection     methods  ,   though     reliable  , are   often     time-consuming     and     may     delay     outbreak     response  .   Molecular     approaches  —  particularly   real-  time   PCR—  offer     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     detection  ,   enabling     timely     intervention     and     source     tracking  .   Building     molecular   testing   capacity     across     national     and   regional   laboratories     strengthens     surveillance     networks     and     enhances     early     warning     systems  . Investments in   infrastructure  ,   training  ,   and     quality     assurance   are   crucial     to     ensure     reliable     and     comparable   data.   Strengthened     capacity     also     improves     compliance     with     international     food     safety     standards  ,   boosts     consumer     confidence  ,   and     helps     reduce     the     incidence     of     Salmonella  -related     foodborne     illness  ,   ultimately     protecting     public     health     and     supporting     economic     resilience  .
